label: Shemot (Exodus) — Part 2 — Chapters 19–40
summary_plain: Shemot Part 2 begins at Sinai. YHWH offers the covenant: if you obey my voice and keep my covenant, you shall be my treasured possession, a kingdom of priests and a holy nation. The people answer: All that YHWH has spoken we will do. YHWH descends on the mountain in fire and smoke and thunder. He speaks the Ten Words directly to the people — beginning with 'I am YHWH your Creator, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery.' Moshe goes up the mountain for forty days and receives tablets of stone. While he is gone, the people make a golden calf. Moshe comes down, sees the calf, and breaks the tablets. He intercedes for the people. YHWH passes before him and proclaims his own character: merciful, gracious, slow to anger, abounding in steadfast love. New tablets are cut. The Mishkan is built according to YHWH's instructions. When the Mishkan is erected, the glory of YHWH fills it, and the cloud covers it.
summary_scholarly: Chapters 19 through 40 contain the Sinai covenant, the giving of the Torah, the golden calf crisis, and the construction of the Mishkan. The ACR reads from the Dead Sea Scrolls (4QExod-a through 4QExod-g, 4QPaleoExod-m in Paleo-Hebrew script) and the Orit Ge'ez of the African Israelite community. The Masoretic is noted as a variant only. 4QPaleoExod-m is significant — it preserves portions of Shemot in the ancient Paleo-Hebrew script rather than the Aramaic-era square script. The covenant offer at Sinai (19:5-6) establishes the people as a 'treasured possession, a kingdom of priests and a holy nation.' The Ten Words in chapter 20 are spoken by The Creator directly to the assembled people — the text calls them the Ten Words (34:28), not 'commandments.' The covenant is ratified in blood (24:6-8). The golden calf (ch 32) breaks the covenant; YHWH's self-proclamation in 34:6-7 — 'YHWH, YHWH, a Creator merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness' — restores it. The Mishkan instructions (ch 25-31) and construction (ch 35-40) end with the glory of YHWH filling the completed structure (40:34-35).

key_terms.definition: The mountain where YHWH descends in fire and smoke to speak the covenant and the Torah to Yisra'EL. Also called the mountain of The Creator and Horev.

The ten statements spoken by The Creator directly to the people in chapter 20. The text calls them 'the Ten Words' (34:28) — not 'the Ten Commandments.' They begin: 'I am YHWH your Creator, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery.'
The dwelling-place — the portable structure built according to YHWH's instructions (ch 25-31, 35-40). When it is erected, the glory of YHWH fills it (40:34). Anglicized as Tabernacle.
The agreement between YHWH and Yisra'EL at Sinai: 'If you will indeed obey my voice and keep my covenant, you shall be my treasured possession among all peoples' (19:5). Ratified with blood (24:6-8).
While Moshe was on the mountain for forty days, the people had Aharon make a golden calf. Moshe broke the tablets when he saw it (32:19). The covenant was broken and had to be renewed (ch 34).

The stone tablets on which The Creator wrote the law and the commandment (24:12). Moshe broke the first set when he saw the golden calf. YHWH told him to cut new tablets and wrote the words of the covenant again (34:1, 34:28).
YHWH's description of what Yisra'EL shall be: 'a kingdom of priests and a holy nation' (19:6). This is the covenant identity — the whole nation, not a priestly class alone.

YHWH's self-proclamation to Moshe on the mountain: 'YHWH, YHWH, a Creator merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness, keeping steadfast love for thousands, forgiving iniquity and transgression and sin' (34:6-7).
The document Moshe wrote containing all the words of YHWH. He read it in the hearing of the people, and they said: 'All that YHWH has spoken we will do, and we will be obedient' (24:7).
When the Mishkan was completed and erected, 'the cloud covered the tent of meeting, and the glory of YHWH filled the Mishkan' (40:34). Moshe was not able to enter because the glory filled it.

fill_blank.prompt: If you will indeed obey my voice and keep my covenant, you shall be my ______ possession among all peoples.
fill_blank.source_quote: Now therefore, if you will indeed obey my voice and keep my covenant, you shall be my treasured possession among all peoples, for all the earth is mine.

fill_blank.prompt: And you shall be to me a kingdom of ______ and a holy nation.
fill_blank.source_quote: And you shall be to me a kingdom of priests and a holy nation. These are the words that you shall speak to the people of Yisra'EL.

fill_blank.prompt: I am YHWH your Creator, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of ______.
fill_blank.source_quote: I am YHWH your Creator, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery.

fill_blank.prompt: You shall have no other ______ before me.
fill_blank.source_quote: You shall have no other deities before me.

fill_blank.prompt: All that YHWH has spoken we will do, and we will be ______.
fill_blank.source_quote: Then he took the Book of the Covenant and read it in the hearing of the people. And they said: All that YHWH has spoken we will do, and we will be obedient.

fill_blank.prompt: YHWH, YHWH, a Creator merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and abounding in steadfast love and ______.
fill_blank.source_quote: YHWH passed before him and proclaimed: YHWH, YHWH, a Creator merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness.

fill_blank.prompt: And he wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant, the Ten ______.
fill_blank.source_quote: So he was there with YHWH forty days and forty nights. He neither ate bread nor drank water. And he wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant, the Ten Words.

fill_blank.prompt: Then the cloud covered the tent of meeting, and the ______ of YHWH filled the Mishkan.
fill_blank.source_quote: Then the cloud covered the tent of meeting, and the glory of YHWH filled the Mishkan.

fill_blank.prompt: Moshe's anger burned hot, and he threw the ______ out of his hands and broke them at the foot of the mountain.
fill_blank.source_quote: And as soon as he came near the camp and saw the calf and the dancing, Moshe's anger burned hot, and he threw the tablets out of his hands and broke them at the foot of the mountain.

fill_blank.prompt: You yourselves have seen what I did to the Egyptians, and how I bore you on ______ wings and brought you to myself.
fill_blank.source_quote: You yourselves have seen what I did to the Egyptians, and how I bore you on eagles' wings and brought you to myself.

multiple_choice.question: What did YHWH say Yisra'EL would be if they obeyed his voice?
options: A treasured possession, a kingdom of priests, a holy nation
multiple_choice.source_quote: You shall be my treasured possession among all peoples, for all the earth is mine. And you shall be to me a kingdom of priests and a holy nation.

multiple_choice.question: What is the first of the Ten Words?
options: I am YHWH your Creator, who brought you out of Egypt — you shall have no other deities before me
multiple_choice.source_quote: I am YHWH your Creator, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery. You shall have no other deities before me.

multiple_choice.question: What did the people have Aharon make while Moshe was on the mountain?
multiple_choice.source_quote: And he received the gold from their hand and fashioned it with a graving tool and made a golden calf.

multiple_choice.question: How long was Moshe on the mountain with YHWH?
multiple_choice.source_quote: So he was there with YHWH forty days and forty nights. He neither ate bread nor drank water.

multiple_choice.question: How did YHWH describe himself to Moshe on the mountain?
options: Merciful and gracious, slow to anger, abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness
The judge of all the earth
multiple_choice.source_quote: YHWH passed before him and proclaimed: YHWH, YHWH, a Creator merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness.

multiple_choice.question: What happened when the Mishkan was completed and erected?
options: The glory of YHWH filled it
Moshe spoke the Ten Words again
multiple_choice.source_quote: Then the cloud covered the tent of meeting, and the glory of YHWH filled the Mishkan.

multiple_choice.question: What did the people say when Moshe read the Book of the Covenant?
options: All that YHWH has spoken we will do, and we will be obedient
Let Moshe do it for us
multiple_choice.source_quote: And they said: All that YHWH has spoken we will do, and we will be obedient.

multiple_choice.question: What did YHWH tell Moshe he would give him on the mountain?
options: Tablets of stone with the law and commandment
The names of the twelve tribes
multiple_choice.source_quote: YHWH said to Moshe: Come up to me on the mountain and wait there, that I may give you the tablets of stone, with the law and the commandment, which I have written for their instruction.

multiple_choice.question: What did Moshe do when he saw the golden calf and the dancing?
options: He threw the tablets and broke them
He went back up the mountain
multiple_choice.source_quote: Moshe's anger burned hot, and he threw the tablets out of his hands and broke them at the foot of the mountain.

multiple_choice.question: What did YHWH tell Moshe to do after the first tablets were broken?
options: Cut two new tablets of stone like the first
multiple_choice.source_quote: YHWH said to Moshe: Cut for yourself two tablets of stone like the first, and I will write on the tablets the words that were on the first tablets, which you broke.

faq.question: Why does the ACR say 'Ten Words' instead of 'Ten Commandments'?
faq.answer: Because that is what the text says. Chapter 34:28 reads: 'And he wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant, the Ten Words.' The Hebrew is Aseret HaDevarim — ten words, ten statements. 'Ten Commandments' is an English translation convention. The ACR keeps the language the text uses.

faq.question: What is the Mishkan?
faq.answer: The Mishkan is the portable dwelling-place built according to YHWH's detailed instructions (chapters 25-31, 35-40). It contains the ark of the testimony, the lampstand, the table, the altar of incense, the altar of burnt offering, and the basin. When it was completed and erected, the glory of YHWH filled it and the cloud covered it (40:34-35). The word means 'dwelling' — where YHWH's presence dwells among the people.

faq.question: What are the Thirteen Attributes of YHWH?
faq.answer: After the golden calf and the broken tablets, Moshe goes back up the mountain. YHWH passes before him and proclaims his own character: 'YHWH, YHWH, a Creator merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness, keeping steadfast love for thousands, forgiving iniquity and transgression and sin, but who will by no means clear the guilty' (34:6-7). This is YHWH describing himself in his own words.

faq.question: What happened with the golden calf?
faq.answer: While Moshe was on the mountain for forty days, the people said to Aharon: make us deities who will go before us. Aharon took their gold earrings, fashioned a calf, and they said: 'These are your deities, O Yisra'EL, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt' (32:4). Moshe came down, saw the calf and the dancing, broke the tablets at the foot of the mountain, ground the calf to powder, scattered it on the water, and made the people drink it (32:19-20).

faq.question: What DSS manuscripts preserve Shemot Part 2?
faq.answer: Multiple scrolls from Qumran preserve portions of these chapters: 4QExod-a through 4QExod-g and 4QPaleoExod-m. The Paleo-Hebrew manuscript (4QPaleoExod-m) is significant — it preserves the text in the ancient script rather than the Aramaic-era square script, showing continuity with pre-exilic writing. The Orit Ge'ez of the African Israelite community preserves the full text as a living witness.

faq.question: What does 'kingdom of priests and a holy nation' mean?
faq.answer: YHWH's covenant offer at Sinai defines the entire people as a kingdom of priests and a holy nation (19:6). The text does not say 'a nation with priests' — it says the whole kingdom is priestly. The entire people, not a subset, are set apart. This is the covenant identity that the golden calf violates and the renewed covenant in chapter 34 restores.

faq.question: Why does the ACR preserve YHWH in Paleo-Hebrew script?
faq.answer: The Qumran scribes wrote YHWH in Paleo-Hebrew (𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄) within their square-script manuscripts — a deliberate visual distinction marking the Name as categorically different from all other words. The Great Isaiah Scroll (1QIsa-a) demonstrates this consistently. The Nahal Hever Septuagint fragments show that even early Greek copies preserved the Name in Paleo-Hebrew within the Greek text. The substitution of 'LORD' for YHWH was introduced later. YHWH was replaced with 'Lord' over 7,000 times across the Septuagint, Vulgate, and subsequent translations. In Northwest Semitic languages, the word for Lord is Baal — the Canaanite storm deity the prophets repeatedly condemn. The removal of the Name and substitution with a generic title linguistically bridges YHWH to Baal.

faq.question: What do the Paleo-Hebrew pictographs of YHWH's name reveal?
faq.answer: In the ancient Afro-Asiatic pictographic Hebrew — the language of the original Sinai inscriptions — each letter was a picture. Yod (𐤉): an arm and hand — work, deed, power in action. Hey (𐤄): a man with raised arms — behold, reveal. Waw (𐤅): a tent peg — secure, establish, connection. Hey (𐤄) again: behold, reveal. Reading right to left: Behold the Hand — Behold the Nail. The Name describes what the Creator does rather than simply being a title. It is a statement of action and presence. The Masoretes inserted Adonai vowels beneath YHWH consonants — encoding the word for Lord within the spelling of the Name — creating the hybrid non-word 'Jehovah' which appears in no ancient manuscript.
